description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base covered in green moss or algae, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof made of weathered shingles, supported by four wooden posts. A horizontal wooden beam spans the top, with a hand crank and spindle mechanism on one side, likely used for drawing water with a rope and bucket. The stonework is thick and robust, forming a protective barrier around the well opening, and the ground around the well is paved with irregular cobblestones. The structure is rustic, with visible wear and natural growth, giving it an old-world, rural charm. No modern features or additional decorative elements are present, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A mossy stone water well with a pitched wooden roof, hand crank, and cobblestone base, exuding a rustic, weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular stone well, mossy surface, pitched roof, and visible hand crank on the left.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the wooden roof supports and the open back of the well, with no crank visible.
description_view_2: Opposite front view similar to view 0, showing the stone well, roof, and crank on the right side.
description_view_3: Rear angled view from the opposite side, focusing on the wooden supports and mossy stonework, with the crank not visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Hand crank and spindle mechanism present on the side, made of wood and metal; no visible bucket.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well opening; wooden roof supported by four posts.
